reasoning: The Lean statement quantifies over an arbitrary group `G` and an arbitrary list `as : List G`, which correctly encodes "for all $a_1, \dots, a_n \in G$". The LHS `as.prod⁻¹` equals $(a_1 a_2 \cdots a_n)^{-1}$. The RHS `(as.reverse.map (λ x => x⁻¹)).prod` first reverses to $[a_n, \dots, a_1]$, maps inversion to get $[a_n^{-1}, \dots, a_1^{-1}]$, then takes the product $a_n^{-1} a_{n-1}^{-1} \cdots a_1^{-1}$. This is logically equivalent to Mathlib's `List.prod_inv_reverse` (which uses `(L.map (·⁻¹)).reverse.prod`) since `map` and `reverse` commute. The empty-list case reduces to $1^{-1} = 1$. The encoding matches the informal statement exactly.
